sample_size.source_quote: 446 (38.9%) were diagnosed with sepsis and/or septic shock

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age ≥65 years, BMI <18.5 kg/m2, hemato-oncologic diseases, ventilator care during ICU stay
mortality_predictors.source_quote: 4 factors (age ≥65 years, BMI 18.5kg/m2, hemato-oncologic diseases as comorbidities, and ventilator care during ICU stay) were signiﬁcant prognostic indicators on 180- and 365-day mortality

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age ≥65 years, BMI <18.5 kg/m2, hemato-oncologic diseases, ventilator care during ICU stay
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The AUCs of the 180-day and 365-day models were 0.713 (95% CI, 0.668–0.756; P<.001) and 0.697 (95% CI, 0.650–0.740; P<.001), respectively. The cut-off score for each of these models for predicting mortality based on the maximum Youden index was ≥1, with the 180-day model having a sensitivity of 73% and a speciﬁcity of 63%

mortality_predictors.confounders_adjusted: Age ≥65 years, BMI <18.5 kg/m2, hemato-oncologic diseases, ventilator care during ICU stay
mortality_predictors.source_quote: The AUCs of the 180-day and 365-day models were 0.713 (95% CI, 0.668–0.756; P<.001) and 0.697 (95% CI, 0.650–0.740; P<.001), respectively. The cut-off score for each of these models for predicting mortality based on the maximum Youden index was ≥1, with the 180-day model having a sensitivity of 73% and a speciﬁcity of 63% and the 365-day model having a sensitivity of 75% and a speciﬁcity of 59%
